metadata.system: Portal de la Transparencia de la Administración General del Estado (AGE)
metadata.procedureName: Ejercicio del derecho de acceso a información pública
metadata.description: Complete list of 25 Spanish government entities for filing transparency access requests. Each entity can be accessed via certificate-based authentication. Agent should use the formEndpoint with user's X.509 certificate.

entities.name: Agencia Española de Protección de Datos

agentIntegrationGuide.overview: This data is designed for certificate-based agents (similar to PideInfo Agent) to autonomously submit transparency requests.
agentIntegrationGuide.architecture: Agent authenticates with user's certificate (OS keychain) → navigates to entity's formEndpoint → fills form → signs submission → downloads proof → sends documents back to your system
implementation: 1. Agent receives entity data (idAmb, baseUrl, formEndpoint, signingEndpoint)
3. Portal redirects to certificate authentication (Cl@ve or FNMT cert picker)
4. User selects certificate from OS keychain (DNI-e, FNMT, or Autofirma)
5. Session established; form is now visible
6. Agent auto-fills form fields (requestor, subject, description)
7. Agent clicks 'Siguiente' → form submission creates idBorr (draft ID)
8. Agent navigates to signingEndpoint with idBorr
10. User re-authenticates (may need to re-enter cert password)
11. Non-repudiation signature created; portal redirects with signValidation UUID
12. Agent extracts expedienteRef from confirmation page
13. Agent downloads PDF justificante (proof of submission)
14. Agent sends documents to your webhook (baseUrl, idAmb, expedienteRef, documents)

entity.name: Ministerio de Transportes y Movilidad Sostenible

certificate_requirements.cert_storage: OS keychain (Keychain on macOS, Credential Manager on Windows, GNOME Keyring on Linux)
certificate_requirements.cert_selection: User selects via browser's native certificate picker; Agent does not handle private keys
certificate_requirements.chain_validation: Portal AGE validates cert chain server-side; Agent does not need X.509 validation logic
